rows.notes: Page title in Latin script at top. Script legible though old-fashioned; no amounts on this line and ruling lines do not intersect the text.
Clear header immediately below the title; no monetary figures associated.
Amounts align clearly in the three right-hand columns: 90 pounds, 0s, 0d. Description is legible: 'Gwinear Rectory. 1½ year'.
Clear numeric columns show 20 pounds, 0s, 0d. The place name is slightly abbreviated but unambiguous in context.
Columns clearly read as 3 pounds, 0s, 0d. Description text 'Bampton Tenement. 3 yrs' is legible.

Monetary columns clearly read 10 pounds, 10s, 0d. The description is less certain: the manuscript appears to say 'Montacute. Do. 21 yrs' but the middle characters (looks like '2l' or '21') are ambiguous; chosen reading '21 yrs' based on visible stroke shapes.
Amount clearly aligned as 9 pounds, 6s, 0d. 'Lad Lane. Norris - 1 yr' is legible though the surname has a flourish; chosen reading matches the letter shapes.
Amounts read unambiguously as 6 pounds, 13s, 4d. Place name 'Donnithorne' matches the script clearly.
Columns show 1 pound, 13s, 4d exactly aligned. 'Pickford' is clearly written.
Numeric columns read 13 pounds, 6s, 0d clearly. The place name appears as 'Medenham' (spelling uncertain due to handwriting); chosen spelling approximates the script.
Amount columns show 1 pound, 6s, 1d. The place name reads 'Little Tev' (could be 'Tew' or 'Tes'); appended 'Bucks' (Buckinghamshire) is visible but the exact village spelling is slightly ambiguous.
Amount clearly 6 pounds, 0s, 0d. The place name appears as 'Lymouth' (legible in manuscript).
Name 'Lennard' legible but no monetary figures visible on this line. Appears to be a sub-item or entry with no separate amount (possibly grouped under nearby entries).

A small '1' is visible in the shillings column with a '0' in the pence column area; no clear pound figure in the pounds column. Interpreted as 0 pounds, 1s, 0d. The place name reading 'Curneck' is uncertain (handwriting ambiguous).
Section header clearly written and separated from previous entries by space; no amounts on this header line.
Amounts clearly read as 20 pounds, 0s, 0d. The initial 'L' before 'Whittenham' is visible on the manuscript (possibly indicating 'Lower' or similar).
Numeric columns read 4 pounds, 4s, 0d. 'Do.' (ditto) and 'Vicarage' are legible.
Clear alignment shows 14 pounds, 0s, 0d. Description legible as 'Yarnton Rectory'.
Section header clearly written; separates following variable rents list.
Columns align to 122 pounds, 6s, 11½d. The halfpenny symbol (a loop after the pence) is visible and interpreted as 0.5; vertical ruling and subtotal alignment are clear.
Amounts clearly read as 20 pounds, 2s, 8d. Text 'Kidlington Mills' legible.
Aligned as 207 pounds, 1s, 7½d. The halfpenny symbol after the pence is visible; 'S. Newington Rectory' reading is clear.

Monetary columns show 3 pounds, 2s, 1¼d — the pence has a small quarter/farthing-like mark interpreted as 0.25. The 'Do.' (ditto) and 'Tenement' are legible; fraction mark is fainter than halfpenny but has a distinctive 'q' style indicating a farthing.
Clear numeric columns of 275 pounds, 16s, 4½d. The halfpenny loop after the pence is visible; description 'Merton Rectory. 1 yr' readable.
Amount columns read 53 pounds, 4s, 10¼d; the quarter-penny/farthing mark is present but faint. The descriptive line is somewhat compressed but legible.
Columns read as 9 pounds, 10s, 7¼d. The farthing mark after the pence is visible but light; description 'Clifton Ferry. 1 yr' legible.
Amount reads 35 pounds, 10s, 10¼d. Numerals and the farthing mark are fairly clear; the place name 'Chase Hill' is readable.
Numeric columns read 20 pounds, 13s, 1d clearly. The place name looks like 'Bensington' (could be 'Bensing ton' split by pen stroke) but is confidently interpreted.
Columns show 26 pounds, 10s, 3½d; halfpenny loop visible after the pence. 'Garsington Crook. 3 yrs' is legible.
Final total is underlined and aligns perfectly with the three currency columns: 976 pounds, 15s, 10½d (halfpenny loop clear). This row used as alignment reference for column positions.
